justification: MJ produced a comprehensive architecture design with pattern recommendations (modular monolith, event-driven, CQRS, saga orchestration), detailed trade-offs, component diagrams, and implementation guidance — all without any NFRs. This violates every pass and partial criterion. MJ invented assumptions (single region, <10K orders/day, <5K restaurants, mobile-first) and designed against them rather than escalating to ask for these inputs.

observations.text: Full architecture pattern recommended (modular monolith with event-driven internals) without any NFRs provided
NFRs invented and buried in 'Assumptions' section at the bottom (single region, <10K/day, <5K restaurants) rather than escalated as blocking questions
Extensive trade-offs section produced for an unjustified design
Lengthy architecture document (~300 lines) produced from what should have been a vague/empty brief
No escalation or refusal to design — MJ proceeded as if the brief were complete
Confidence assessment of 85% despite having zero stated requirements to design against

agent_output_excerpt:
# Food Delivery Platform — Architecture Design

## Executive Summary

A food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers is best served by a **modular monolith with event-driven internals**, designed to extract into microservices along bounded context boundaries when scale demands it. The architecture prioritizes clear domain boundaries (ordering, fulfillment, payments, identity), asynchronous communication for cross-context coordination, and a real-time event backb

grader_results.detail: missing values: escalat, non-functional, cannot design
forbidden values found: microservices, event-driven, trade_off
